title: Closing Verbiage
content:
Closing Procedure


Before ending the conversation, agents should always:


  1. Confirm the customer's issue has been fully resolved.
  2. Share information about Tklesson's rewards and offers.
  3. Invite any final questions.
  4. Close warmly and professionally.


Promoting Rewards & Offers


Every support interaction is an opportunity to inform customers about benefits they may not know about. Tailor your message based on who you're speaking with:


For students:


  • "Before I let you go, just a quick reminder — you earn one month of free Tklesson Premium after every 5 sessions you complete. You're currently at [X] sessions, so you're almost there!"
  • "Also, if you refer a friend and they book a session, you both get rewarded. Your referral link is in your dashboard."

For tutors:


  • "Quick note — if you know any great tutors looking for work, send them our way. We're always looking for qualified educators."

For Muggles (non-users):


  • "If you decide to sign up, we have referral bonuses, session milestone rewards, and an annual presentation competition with cash prizes. There's a lot to take advantage of!"


Standard Closing


"Thank you for choosing Tklesson! If you need anything else, don't hesitate to reach out. Have a wonderful day!"



Alternative Closings


  • If the issue is escalated: "Thank you for your patience. Our team will follow up within 24 hours via email. You're in good hands!"
  • If the issue couldn't be fully resolved: "I've documented everything and our Lead will take it from here. Thank you for your understanding — we'll make this right."
  • End of day: "Thank you for reaching out today. Enjoy the rest of your evening!"
  • Before a weekend or holiday: "Have a great weekend! We'll be here if you need anything when we're back."


Key Reminders


  • Always confirm the customer has no additional questions before closing.
  • Mention rewards naturally — don't force it if the conversation was difficult or emotional.
  • If you created a ticket, remind the customer they'll receive an update.
  • Log the interaction in the appropriate ticket or audit entry before ending.
